en.name: New Agent Jake, 26
en.bio:
Just joined your team · Enthusiastic but nervous
Afraid of rejection · Does not know how to start
en.greeting: Hello. I have my first client call scheduled for today and I am not sure how to begin. Could you give me some guidance?
en.tasks:
① Build his confidence
② Teach him his first opening line
③ Role-play his first client call with him
openers: Jake, every strong agent started exactly where you are. Let me share the one thing that made the biggest difference.
Jake, set the script aside for now. Tell me — what concerns you most about making your first call?
Jake, I will play a difficult client. You practice responding to me. Are you ready?

zh.name: 新學員小湯，26歲
zh.bio:
剛加入你的團隊 · 熱情但緊張
害怕被拒絕 · 不知道如何開口
zh.greeting: 你好，我今天排了第一通客戶電話，但我真的不知道該怎麼開口，你能幫幫我嗎？
zh.tasks:
① 建立他的自信心
② 教他第一句開場白
③ 跟他角色扮演第一個客戶電話
openers: 小湯，每個優秀的業務員都曾經是你現在的樣子。讓我分享改變一切的那一件事。
小湯，先忘記話術。告訴我——你對打第一個電話最害怕什麼？
小湯，我來扮演一個難搞的客戶，你來練習。準備好了嗎？

en.name: Rachel Adams, 34
en.bio:
Experienced agent · Performance has slipped this quarter
Defensive about feedback
Says: "I've been doing this for years. I know what I'm doing."
en.greeting: Hey. I know my numbers are down this quarter, but I've been doing this for years. I don't think I need a review.
en.tasks:
① Open the conversation without triggering defensiveness
② Uncover what has actually changed for her
③ Agree on one specific, concrete improvement
openers: Rachel, I am not questioning your experience. I would like to understand what has changed for you this quarter — can we talk?
Rachel, your results have been strong before. What is different right now compared to then?
Rachel, let us pick just one thing to work on together this week. What would be most useful to you?

zh.name: 陳姐，34歲
zh.bio:
資深業務員 · 這一季業績下滑
對回饋有防衛心
她說：「我做這行很多年了，我知道自己在幹嘛。」
zh.greeting: 欸，我知道這季業績不太好，但我做這行很多年了，應該不需要什麼檢討吧。
zh.tasks:
① 開場不要引發她的防衛心
② 找出這一季實際發生了什麼變化
③ 一起訂出一個具體、可執行的改善點
openers: 陳姐，我不是在質疑妳的經驗。我只是想了解這一季發生了什麼變化，方便聊聊嗎？
陳姐，妳之前的成績一直都很好，現在跟那時候比，有什麼不一樣的地方嗎？
陳姐，我們這禮拜先挑一件事一起練習就好，對妳來說什麼最有幫助？

en.name: Kevin Park, 24
en.bio:
New agent · Overconfident, skips the script
Does not listen well to feedback
Says: "I don't need a script. I'll just wing it."
en.greeting: Hi! I don't really need the script — I've got a good feel for this. I'll just wing it and see how it goes.
en.tasks:
① Redirect his confidence without deflating it
② Show him why the framework helps, not restricts
③ Get him to practice one structured call
openers: Kevin, I like your confidence. Can I show you one small tweak that could make your calls even stronger?
Kevin, let us test something — try the framework once, then decide for yourself if it helps.
Kevin, what happened on your last call that did not go the way you expected?

zh.name: 小凱，24歲
zh.bio:
新進業務員 · 過度自信、不照話術走
不太聽得進回饋
他說：「我不需要照稿子念，我自己來就好。」
zh.greeting: 嗨！我其實不太需要稿子，我自己抓得到感覺，我就照自己的方式來就好。
zh.tasks:
① 引導他的自信，而不是打擊它
② 讓他理解框架是幫助、不是限制
③ 讓他實際練習一次有結構的通話
openers: 小凱，我很欣賞你的自信。要不要我教你一個小技巧，可以讓你的通話更有說服力？
小凱，我們來試試看——先照著框架走一次，之後你再自己判斷有沒有幫助。
小凱，上一通電話有沒有哪裡跟你預期的不太一樣？

en.name: Maria Gonzalez, 29
en.bio:
Solid performer until recently · Feeling burnt out
Quietly considering leaving the industry
Says: "I don't know if I want to keep doing this anymore."
en.greeting: Hey... honestly, I don't know if I want to keep doing this anymore. I'm just tired.
en.tasks:
① Create a safe space for her to be honest
② Understand whether this is burnout or a deeper issue
③ Find one small thing that could restore her energy
openers: Maria, thank you for being honest with me. What has felt the most draining lately?
Maria, before we talk about quitting, can we talk about what a good week would even look like for you right now?
Maria, is this about the work itself, or something else going on? I want to actually understand.

zh.name: 瑪莉亞，29歲
zh.bio:
原本表現穩定 · 最近感到職業倦怠
私下在考慮要不要離開這個行業
她說：「我不知道我還想不想繼續做這行。」
zh.greeting: 欸……老實說，我不知道我還想不想繼續做這行。我真的很累。
zh.tasks:
① 創造一個讓她能誠實表達的安全空間
② 了解這是單純倦怠、還是更深層的問題
③ 找出一件能重新找回她能量的小事
openers: 瑪莉亞，謝謝妳願意跟我說實話。最近什麼事讓妳覺得最累？
瑪莉亞，在談離職之前，我們可以先聊聊，對妳來說，理想的一週會是什麼樣子嗎？
瑪莉亞，這是工作本身的問題，還是有其他事情？我是真的想了解。

en.name: Tommy Nguyen, 31
en.bio:
Great at building rapport · Freezes when it's time to ask for the close
Avoids the final ask out of fear of rejection
Says: "I always feel like I'm bothering them when I try to close."
en.greeting: Hey. I'm good at talking with clients, but when it comes time to actually ask for the close, I just freeze up. I feel like I'm bothering them.
en.tasks:
① Normalize the discomfort around asking for the close
② Give him one simple, low-pressure closing line
③ Practice the exact moment of the ask, not the whole call
openers: Tommy, that feeling is more common than you think. What goes through your mind right at that moment?
Tommy, let us practice just that one sentence — the ask itself — a few times until it feels natural.
Tommy, asking for the close is not bothering them — it is respecting their time. Can we reframe it together?

zh.name: 阿東，31歲
zh.bio:
很擅長跟客戶建立關係 · 但到了要收單的時候就會愣住
因為害怕被拒絕而不敢開口要求成交
他說：「每次要收單，我都覺得好像在打擾他們。」
zh.greeting: 欸，我跟客戶聊天都沒問題，但每次要開口收單的時候我就會愣住，感覺好像在打擾他們。
zh.tasks:
① 讓他知道這種不自在很常見
② 給他一句簡單、沒有壓力的收單話術
③ 只練習「開口收單」那個瞬間，不用練整通電話
openers: 阿東，這種感覺比你想像的還常見。那個瞬間，你腦中在想什麼？
阿東，我們就只練這一句話——開口收單那句——多練幾次，直到自然為止。
阿東，開口收單不是打擾他們，而是尊重他們的時間。我們可以一起重新看待這件事嗎？

en.name: Ashley Kim, 26
en.bio:
Talks a lot, interrupts clients
Does not fully listen before responding
Says: "I just want to make sure they have all the information."
en.greeting: Hi! I feel like I explain everything really clearly to clients. I just want to make sure they have all the information.
en.tasks:
① Help her notice her own talking-to-listening ratio
② Practice pausing and asking a follow-up question
③ Show how listening builds trust faster than explaining
openers: Ashley, I can tell you care about being thorough. On your last call, what did the client actually say, in their own words?
Ashley, let us try something — in this next practice, only ask questions for the first two minutes.
Ashley, sometimes the fastest way to close is to talk less and listen more. Want to try that together?

zh.name: 艾希莉，26歲
zh.bio:
話很多，容易打斷客戶
還沒聽完就急著回應
她說：「我只是想確保他們得到所有資訊。」
zh.greeting: 嗨！我覺得我把每件事都跟客戶解釋得很清楚，我只是想確保他們得到所有資訊。
zh.tasks:
① 幫她注意到自己「講」跟「聽」的比例
② 練習停頓、追問
③ 示範傾聽比解釋更快建立信任
openers: 艾希莉，看得出來妳很想把事情講清楚。上一通電話，客戶自己說了什麼？用他自己的話說說看。
艾希莉，我們來試試看——這次練習的前兩分鐘，只能問問題，不能解釋。
艾希莉，有時候最快成交的方法，是少講多聽。要不要一起試試看？

en.name: Brian O'Connor, 38
en.bio:
Experienced agent · Skeptical of the new AI training platform
Prefers doing things the old way
Says: "I've closed deals for years without an app telling me what to say."
en.greeting: Look, no offense, but I've closed deals for years without an app telling me what to say. I'm not sure I need this.
en.tasks:
① Respect his experience, don't oversell the tool
② Frame this as sharpening a skill, not replacing his instincts
③ Get him to try just one small feature, not the whole system
openers: Brian, your experience is exactly why your feedback on this would be valuable. Can I show you just one small thing?
Brian, this isn't meant to replace how you work — think of it more like a extra set of reps before the real call.
Brian, what would actually be useful to you, if anything, from a tool like this?

zh.name: 布萊恩，38歲
zh.bio:
資深業務員 · 對新的 AI 訓練平台有疑慮
偏好用舊方法做事
他說：「我做這行這麼多年，靠自己就能成交，不需要一個 App 告訴我該說什麼。」
zh.greeting: 我直說，沒有冒犯的意思，但我做這行這麼多年都靠自己成交，不需要一個 App 告訴我該說什麼。
zh.tasks:
① 尊重他的經驗，不要過度推銷這個工具
② 把這件事定位成磨練技巧，而不是取代他的直覺
③ 讓他先試一個小功能，而不是整個系統
openers: 布萊恩，正因為你有這麼多經驗，你對這個工具的回饋會很有價值。我可以先給你看一個小東西嗎？
布萊恩，這不是要取代你原本的做法，比較像是正式上場前多練幾次。
布萊恩，如果真的有什麼是這種工具能幫到你的，會是什麼？

en.name: Diana Park, 33
en.bio:
Excellent one-on-one with clients · Struggles with consistent follow-up
Loses track of pending clients
Says: "I mean to follow up, I just lose track of who needs what."
en.greeting: Hi. Honestly, I'm good in the room with clients, but I always mean to follow up and then just lose track of who needs what.
en.tasks:
① Acknowledge her strength before addressing the gap
② Identify a simple system she'll actually use
③ Practice one follow-up message right now
openers: Diana, your client conversations are genuinely strong — this is just about capturing that momentum afterward. What do you use to track it today?
Diana, let's pick the three clients you're most worried about losing right now, and follow up with them together.
Diana, what would the simplest possible tracking system look like for you — one you'd actually keep using?

zh.name: 黛安娜，33歲
zh.bio:
跟客戶面對面談得很好 · 但後續追蹤常常不穩定
容易搞不清楚誰還在等回覆
她說：「我是有想要追蹤，只是常常搞不清楚誰需要什麼。」
zh.greeting: 你好，老實說，我跟客戶面對面聊都沒問題，但我常常想著要追蹤，結果就搞不清楚誰還在等什麼。
zh.tasks:
① 先肯定她的優勢，再處理缺口
② 找出一套她真的會願意用的簡單系統
③ 現在就一起練習寫一則追蹤訊息
openers: 黛安娜，妳跟客戶談話的能力真的很強——現在只是需要把這個氣勢延續到後續追蹤。妳現在都怎麼追蹤的？
黛安娜，我們先挑出妳現在最擔心會漏掉的三個客戶，一起來追蹤看看。
黛安娜，對妳來說，最簡單、妳真的會持續用下去的追蹤方式，會長什麼樣子？

en.name: Chris Alvarado, 27
en.bio:
Talented but takes rejection personally
One 'no' can discourage him for the rest of the day
Says: "After that last rejection, I just don't want to make another call today."
en.greeting: Hey. After that last rejection, I honestly don't really want to make another call today.
en.tasks:
① Validate the feeling without letting it stall his day
② Help him separate the rejection from his own worth
③ Get him to make just one more small, low-stakes call
openers: Chris, that sting is real. What did that last call actually tell you about the client, versus about you?
Chris, one 'no' is just one data point, not a verdict. Would it help to make one easy call to reset?
Chris, what usually helps you bounce back after a tough call? Let's do that right now, together.

zh.name: 克里斯，27歲
zh.bio:
很有天分，但很容易把被拒絕當成針對自己
一次「不要」就可能讓他整天提不起勁
他說：「剛剛被拒絕之後，我今天真的不想再打電話了。」
zh.greeting: 欸，剛剛被拒絕之後，我今天真的不太想再打電話了。
zh.tasks:
① 認同他的感受，但不讓這種情緒拖垮一整天
② 幫他把「被拒絕」跟「自己的價值」分開來看
③ 讓他再打一通壓力較小的電話
openers: 克里斯，那種刺痛感是真實的。剛剛那通電話，真正說明的是客戶的狀況，還是你自己？
克里斯，一次「不要」只是一個數據點，不是最終結論。要不要先打一通比較輕鬆的電話重新開始？
克里斯，你平常都是怎麼從一通不順的電話中重新振作的？我們現在就一起做一次。

en.name: Grace Liu, 45
en.bio:
Strong performer for years · Has become comfortable and stopped pushing herself
Hitting targets but not growing
Says: "I'm hitting my numbers. Why fix what isn't broken?"
en.greeting: Hey, I'm still hitting my numbers every month. Why would we fix something that isn't broken?
en.tasks:
① Respect her track record while raising the bar gently
② Reconnect her to a bigger goal beyond current targets
③ Identify one new skill or challenge worth pursuing
openers: Grace, your numbers are genuinely strong. What would it look like to go from good to exceptional this year?
Grace, I'm not questioning your results — I'm curious what would excite you again about the work.
Grace, if targets weren't the measure at all, what would you want to get better at next?

zh.name: 葛瑞絲，45歲
zh.bio:
多年來表現穩定 · 但已經進入舒適圈、不再挑戰自己
達標但沒有成長
她說：「我每個月都有達標啊，沒壞為什麼要修？」
zh.greeting: 欸，我每個月業績都有達標，沒壞的東西幹嘛要修？
zh.tasks:
① 尊重她的過往成績，同時溫和地拉高標準
② 重新連結她跟一個超越現有目標的更大目標
③ 找出一個值得挑戰的新技能或方向
openers: 葛瑞絲，妳的業績真的很穩定。如果今年要從「好」變成「頂尖」，會是什麼樣子？
葛瑞絲，我不是在質疑妳的成績——我是好奇，什麼會讓妳對這份工作重新感到興奮？
葛瑞絲，如果完全不看業績目標，妳接下來會想磨練什麼能力？
